back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: A man, when he makes a special vow concerning the valuation of persons to the Lord,
then your valuation shall be: for a male from twenty years old to sixty years old, your valuation shall be fifty shekels of silver, by the sanctuary shekel.
And if it is a female, your valuation shall be thirty shekels.
And if from five years old to twenty years old, your valuation shall be: the male twenty shekels, and the female ten shekels.
And if from one month to five years old, your valuation shall be: the male five shekels of silver, and the female your valuation three shekels of silver.
And if from sixty years old and upward—if a male, your valuation shall be fifteen shekels, and the female ten shekels.

But if he is too poor for your valuation, then he shall present him before the priest, and the priest shall value him; according to what the hand of the one vowing can afford, the priest shall value him.
And if it is an animal from which they may offer an offering to the Lord, all that he gives from it to the Lord shall be holy.
He shall not replace it and shall not exchange it, good for bad or bad for good. But if exchanging he does exchange animal for animal, then it and its substitute shall be holy.
But if any unclean animal, from which they do not offer an offering to the Lord, then he shall present the animal before the priest.
And the priest shall value it, between good and bad. According to your valuation, O priest, so shall it be.
And if he indeed redeems it, then he shall add a fifth of it to your valuation.
And a man, when he consecrates his house as holy to the Lord, the priest shall value it, between good and bad. As the priest values it, so shall it stand.
But if the one consecrating redeems his house, then he shall add a fifth of the silver of your valuation upon it, and it shall be his.
And if a man consecrates from the field of his possession to the Lord, then your valuation shall be according to its seed: a homer of barley seed at fifty shekels of silver.
If from the Year of the Jubilee he consecrates his field, according to your valuation it shall stand.

But if after the Jubilee he consecrates his field, then the priest shall calculate for him the silver according to the years that remain until the Year of the Jubilee, and it shall be reduced from your valuation.
And if redeeming the one consecrating it redeems the field, then he shall add a fifth of the silver of your valuation upon it, and it shall stand for him.
But if he does not redeem the field, and if he sells the field to another man, it shall no longer be redeemed.
And the field shall be, when it goes out in the Jubilee, holy to the Lord, like a field of devotion. To the priest shall be his possession.
And if he consecrates to the Lord a field of his purchase, which is not from the field of his possession,
then the priest shall calculate for him the amount of your valuation up to the Year of the Jubilee, and he shall give your valuation on that day—holy to the Lord.
In the Year of the Jubilee the field shall return to the one from whom he bought it, to the one whose possession of the land it is.
And every valuation of yours shall be by the sanctuary shekel; twenty gerahs shall the shekel be.
Only a firstborn that is a firstborn to the Lord among animals—a man shall not consecrate it; whether ox or sheep, it is the Lord's.
But if it is among the unclean animals, then he shall redeem it according to your valuation and add a fifth of it to it; and if it is not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to your valuation.

Only any devoted thing that a man devotes to the Lord from all that is his—from man and beast and from the field of his possession—shall not be sold and shall not be redeemed. Every devoted thing is most holy to the Lord.
Every devoted person who is devoted from mankind shall not be ransomed; he shall surely be put to death.
And every tithe of the land, from the seed of the land, from the fruit of the tree, is to the Lord; it is holy to the Lord.
And if redeeming a man redeems from his tithe, a fifth of it he shall add upon it.
And every tithe of the herd and flock, all that passes under the rod—the tenth shall be holy to the Lord.
He shall not inquire between good and bad, and he shall not exchange it. And if exchanging he exchanges it, then it and its substitute shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.
These are the commandments that the Lord commanded Moses for the sons of Israel on Mount Sinai.
